indication: Acute worsening dyspnea in patient with heart failure and atrial fibrillation, evaluate for pulmonary embolism. CT angiography deferred due to CKD Stage 3b (eGFR 38).
technique: Ventilation imaging was performed following inhalation of Tc-99m DTPA aerosol. Perfusion imaging was performed following intravenous administration of Tc-99m MAA (4 mCi) in 8 standard projections.

findings.ventilation: Homogeneous ventilation throughout both lungs. No focal ventilation defects. Mild central airway deposition consistent with turbulent flow.
findings.perfusion: Homogeneous perfusion throughout both lungs. No segmental or subsegmental perfusion defects identified. Mild heterogeneity in the bases bilaterally, likely related to dependent atelectasis and elevated hemidiaphragms.
findings.matching: No ventilation-perfusion mismatches identified. The mild basilar perfusion heterogeneity is matched on ventilation images.
findings.chestXray: Concurrent chest radiograph demonstrates cardiomegaly, mild pulmonary vascular congestion, and small bilateral pleural effusions consistent with heart failure.

impression: Low probability for pulmonary embolism. No segmental or subsegmental ventilation-perfusion mismatches identified.
Mild bilateral basilar perfusion heterogeneity is matched on ventilation images and likely reflects dependent atelectasis and pleural effusions in the setting of heart failure.
Cardiomegaly and findings of heart failure on concurrent chest radiograph.
recommendations: Dyspnea likely related to heart failure exacerbation rather than pulmonary embolism
Consider diuretic adjustment and optimization of heart failure therapy
comparison: No prior V/Q scan available for comparison.
